reviews.comment:
Eng444 was a great course.  It's really, really hard but I enjoyed it.  Dr. Thomas is supper intelligent and willing to help students when asked.

Attendance isn't mandatory, but you better be there to get the info anyway.
One of the best professors ever !

Dr. Thomas is one of the smartest professors in the entire university, but he remains very humble and approachable. He helped me apply for scholarships, start special projects, and wrote me letters of recommendation. His courses are challenging in just the right way.
He usually showed up late after the first 3 weeks, not a lot of homework and in-class activities. Easy to get an A if you pay attention and turn in work on time.
His class is easy to get A, just do all the assignments. He barely reads them actually.
reviews.ratingTags: Graded by few things--Clear grading criteria

reviews.comment: one of the worst profs I've ever had. Very scatter brained. Lectures usually had no point to them. Never answers emails. Over explains making things more difficult than they need to be. No point to the class. Is obsessed with the fact he has a linguistics degree.
reviews.ratingTags: Lots of homework--Skip class? You won't pass.

reviews.comment: Worst English teacher I've ever had. Lectures were varied but never applied to what we were working on. Suppose to assign an essay a month but saved them all until after spring break, we were very rushed. Never checks his email or responds. Directions are unclear and useless. Wasted lots of time.
reviews.ratingTags: Participation matters--Graded by few things--So many papers

reviews.comment: Easy A, just do the work. Lectures were never the same he had a variety of content that was easy to understand and write about. We visited museums on campus and had a few casual group presentations based on that. Whole grade is based on the final portfolio that includes all the little writing assignments throughout the semester. Fun class overall.
